tldr: Lucide and Phosphor Icons are both open-source SVG icon families with framework packages, so migration is mostly a rename-and-reimport exercise. The main work is mapping icon names and choosing weight variants Phosphor offers that Lucide does not.
why_switch: Need multiple weight variants (thin, light, regular, bold, fill, duotone)
Want a larger catalog (1200+ icons vs Lucide's set)
Prefer Flutter and Figma library coverage in addition to web
Express brand voice through weight rather than single style
what_to_export: Inventory of every Lucide icon name used
Framework package in use (react, vue, svelte, angular)
Size, color, and stroke-width props per usage
Any custom-styled or remapped icon components
Design token mapping from token name to Lucide icon
Accessibility labels associated with each icon
Bundle import strategy (named imports vs barrel)
Tests or snapshots referencing icon component names
export_method: There is no service export — Lucide is a static library. Run a codebase grep for imports from lucide-react, lucide-vue-next, lucide-svelte, or lucide-angular and produce a CSV with: file path, icon PascalCase name, props passed (size, color, strokeWidth, className), and the surface where it appears. Capture the design-system token-to-icon mapping if you maintain one (typically a JSON or TS file). Note any icons you have wrapped in higher-level components (e.g. a DSIcon abstraction) — those become the migration choke points. Save current visual snapshots of representative screens so a reviewer can confirm parity after switching weights. If you import via a barrel file, list each icon once; if you import per-file, the grep gives you the per-occurrence count for impact sizing.
import_method: Install the Phosphor package for your framework (e.g. @phosphor-icons/react, @phosphor-icons/vue, phosphor-flutter, or the Figma library). Decide on a default weight that matches Lucide's stroke aesthetic — Phosphor's regular weight is the closest visual analog. Replace imports module-by-module: rename the Lucide PascalCase name to the Phosphor equivalent (most common icons exist on both sides under similar names), and pass size/color via Phosphor's props (size, color, weight). Where Lucide passed strokeWidth, pick a Phosphor weight instead. Update your design-system token map so the same token now resolves to a Phosphor component. Run visual regression on representative screens; adjust weights per surface if needed. Keep accessibility labels by passing aria-label on the wrapping element.

concept_mapping.note: Many overlap; some need manual remap

gotchas: Icon names do not all match one-to-one; build a rename map and review unmatched names manually
Phosphor's regular weight is slightly heavier than Lucide; QA visually before shipping
Multiple weights can balloon bundle size — stick to one or two unless you need contrast
Duotone variants render two colors and may need theming changes for dark mode
Tests and snapshots referencing Lucide component names will need updating
Tree-shaking depends on per-icon imports; avoid barrel re-exports that pull the whole set

email.subject: Migrating icon library from Lucide to Phosphor Icons
email.body:
Hi team,

We are migrating our icon library from Lucide to Phosphor Icons over the next 14 days. The driver is access to multiple weight variants (regular, bold, fill, duotone) and a larger catalog, both useful as we expand the product.

The work is mostly mechanical: rename imports, map icon names, and replace strokeWidth with Phosphor's weight prop. We will keep size, color, and aria-label conventions identical. Visual regression will run on representative screens before merging.

If you own a screen with custom icon styling or a hand-rolled icon wrapper, please flag it so we can review together. The design-system token map will be updated centrally.

No user-visible behavior changes are expected.

Thanks,
Your customer
